 {"id":"bd-bfsm","title":"Implement classDef directive parsing and application","description":"# classDef Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
classDef defines reusable CSS-like style classes that can be applied to nodes:
\`\`\`
classDef important fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
classDef default fill:#fff
class nodeA,nodeB important
\`\`\`

Supported style properties: fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-dasharray, color, font-size, font-weight, font-family, background, border-radius, padding.

## Current State in frankenmermaid
- The \`class nodeA className\` syntax IS parsed (mermaid_parser.rs) and classes are stored on IrNode.classes
- The \`classDef\` syntax itself is NOT parsed — no style definitions are captured
- The SVG renderer does not apply any class-based styling to nodes

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser** (mermaid_parser.rs): Parse \`classDef className prop:value,prop:value\` lines. Store in a new \`IrStyleDef\` type in MermaidDiagramIr (add to fm-core).
2. **IR** (fm-core/src/lib.rs): Add \`style_defs: BTreeMap<String, IrStyleDef>\` to MermaidDiagramIr. IrStyleDef contains fill, stroke, stroke_width, stroke_dasharray, color, etc.
3. **SVG Renderer** (fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs): When rendering a node, look up its classes in style_defs and apply fill/stroke/etc. as inline SVG attributes.
4. **Terminal Renderer**: No visual impact (terminal doesn't support colors in basic mode).

## Test Plan
- Parse classDef with single property
- Parse classDef with multiple comma-separated properties
- Apply class to node and verify SVG attributes
- Verify default classDef applies to unclassed nodes
- Verify classDef with quoted values","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.353430251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:18:47.961647754Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:18:47.961366301Z","close_reason":"classDef parsing already existed via extract_style_directives; added populate_structured_styles() call in ir_builder::finish() to populate style_defs and per-node/edge inline_style. Enhanced style directive to support comma-separated node IDs. Added 8 parser integration tests. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:22.387976601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"},{"id":184,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"CROSS-DIAGRAM NOTE: classDef is supported in mermaid-js for flowchart, class, state, and potentially other diagram types. The parser implementation should handle classDef lines in the generic pre-processing phase (before diagram-type-specific parsing) so it works across all types. The IR's style_defs field is on MermaidDiagramIr which is shared by all diagram types.","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bium","title":"Implement flowchart parallel edge syntax (A & B --> C & D)","description":"# Flowchart Parallel Edge Syntax

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
flowchart LR
  A & B --> C & D
\`\`\`
This creates 4 edges: A→C, A→D, B→C, B→D. The & operator allows declaring multiple source/target nodes in a single edge statement.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. The & character is not recognized in edge declarations.
- Mermaid parser's edge AST only supports single source → single target.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: In parse_edge_statement_asts(), before looking for arrow operators, split node references on '&'. If either side contains '&', expand to multiple FlowAst::Edge entries (cartesian product of sources × targets).
2. **Tests**: A & B --> C (2 edges), A --> B & C (2 edges), A & B --> C & D (4 edges), single node (no &, unchanged behavior).

## Considerations
- The & operator is only valid in flowchart, not other diagram types
- Must handle whitespace: 'A&B' vs 'A & B' vs 'A &B'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:14.744584229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T01:31:57.010578087Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T01:31:57.010075205Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-bium","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### Integration Tests

- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)

 {"id":"bd-ckwu","title":"Sequence follow-up: layout/render participant group boxes from sequence_meta","description":"# Goal
Sequence `box ... end` groups are parsed into `IrSequenceMeta.participant_groups` but never laid out or rendered in the current pipeline, so the closed parity bead is not actually complete.

# Scope
- Convert `sequence_meta.participant_groups` into deterministic layout geometry in `fm-layout`.
- Render those group boxes across SVG, terminal, and canvas.
- Preserve deterministic ordering and nested-group behavior.
- Add focused tests proving `box` syntax produces visible output, not just parser metadata.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Sequence `box` groups appear in layout/render output across all three renderers.
- Geometry is deterministic and encloses the intended participant headers/lifelines.
- Focused parser/layout/render regression tests cover the feature.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:38:52.401186772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T17:50:52.018035957Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T17:50:52.017538713Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","render","sequence"]}
-{"id":"bd-cpgf","title":"Add .gitignore patterns for audit scratch files and commit cleanup","description":"## Problem
Working tree shows ~25 untracked audit/scratch files cluttering git status:
- clippy_*.txt (8 files), ubs_*.txt, ubs_*.jsonl, rust-bug-scan.txt, new_ubs.txt
- Per-crate lint outputs: layout_clippy.txt, parser_clippy.txt, term_clippy.txt, cli_clippy.txt
- Python __pycache__/ dirs in tests/ and scripts/
- patch.diff, run_test.sh, pass*.jsonl
- B[🚀 (zero-byte mojibake parser test garbage)
- .beads/issues.jsonl.rej (resolved 2026-04-09, removed)

## Status (partially done in this session)
.gitignore has been UPDATED with patterns for all the above categories. The patterns are:
- clippy_*.txt, ubs_*.txt, ubs_*.jsonl, rust-bug-scan.txt, new_ubs.txt
- layout_clippy.txt, parser_clippy.txt, term_clippy.txt, cli_clippy.txt
- __pycache__/, patch.diff, .beads/*.rej, run_test.sh, pass*.jsonl, B[*

## Remaining work
1. Commit the .gitignore changes.
2. Verify git status is clean after the ignore patterns take effect.
3. If any tracked files need to be un-tracked (e.g., files that were previously committed but should now be ignored), handle with `git rm --cached`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `git status` shows no untracked audit/scratch files.
- .gitignore patterns are committed to main.
- Future clippy/UBS audit outputs are automatically ignored.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:59.876755910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T17:28:37.840626256Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cpgf","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:05.483043257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ct6f","title":"Sankey: Implement flow color gradients and value-proportional rendering","description":"# Sankey Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Flow bands should have color gradients (source color → target color)
- Flow width should be proportional to value (currently approximated)
- Node labels should be positioned outside the flow for readability
- Interactive hover highlighting (WASM only)

## Implementation Plan
1. Implement gradient fills on flow bands using linearGradient defs
2. Ensure flow band width exactly proportional to value/total_flow
3. Position node labels to the left of first-column nodes and right of last-column nodes
4. Add data attributes for WASM hover interaction","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:10.808759311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T03:23:21.089716165Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T03:23:21.089436546Z","close_reason":"Added sankey-node CSS class to source/target nodes for styling. Flow value already captured as edge label.  {"id":"bd-kugr","title":"Restore build-wasm.sh wasm size budget after fm-wasm package regeneration","description":"bd-b0pc.3 fixed the npm package branding and the wasm32 build blockers, but rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_fm_wasm ./build-wasm.sh still exits nonzero because the generated pkg/frankenmermaid_bg.wasm is over the enforced gzip budget.

Current evidence:
- build now completes wasm-pack + wasm-opt and regenerates branded npm artifacts successfully
- final size gate reports raw wasm 1520341 bytes and gzipped wasm 602381 bytes
- budget is 512000 bytes, so the run fails only at the post-build size check

Scope:
1. Determine which recent fm-wasm/fm-layout changes account for the oversized browser bundle
2. Reduce the wasm bundle back under the existing 500 KiB gzip budget or intentionally revise the budget with evidence
3. Keep the branded npm package metadata and README regeneration added in bd-b0pc.3 intact
4. Verify ./build-wasm.sh exits 0 again via rch exec -- ...

Cross-reference:
- surfaced while finishing bd-b0pc.3
- blocks a fully clean wasm packaging verification run","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T23:39:46.941741509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:30:00.867518105Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:30:00.867206226Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","packaging","performance","wasm"]}
 {"id":"bd-ma11","title":"EPIC: Eliminate Hardcoded Constants and Make Rendering Theme-Aware","description":"# EPIC: Eliminate Hardcoded Constants and Make Rendering Theme-Aware

## Background
A comprehensive audit across all sessions identified two categories of hardcoded values that undermine correctness:

1. **Hardcoded colors in renderers** that bypass the theme system, causing poor/invisible output on dark, high-contrast, or custom themes. These are functional bugs — the output is wrong for ~60% of theme presets.

2. **Hardcoded layout dimensions** (chart sizes, margins, spacing) that should be configurable or computed from content. These produce acceptable output for typical diagrams but fail to adapt to unusual content (very long labels, many data points, small terminals).

## Why This Matters
frankenmermaid ships with 10 theme presets (Default, Dark, Forest, Neutral, Corporate, Neon, Pastel, HighContrast, Monochrome, Blueprint). Any hardcoded color that works on the Default theme but fails on Dark/Neon/HighContrast is a bug that affects every user who switches themes. The theme system exists specifically to avoid this — the hardcoded values are bypassing it.

For layout constants, the issue is less acute but still important: pie charts with 20+ slices, quadrant charts with long axis labels, and gantt charts with many sections all need their layout to adapt rather than using fixed 400px dimensions.

## Scope
- **In scope**: All hardcoded hex colors, all hardcoded chart dimensions, all hardcoded spacing that should come from LayoutSpacing or config
- **Out of scope**: Deliberate visual design constants (rounded corners, dash patterns, opacity values, font size multipliers) that are independent of theme/content

## Success Criteria
- Zero hardcoded hex color strings in SVG/Canvas renderers outside of theme.rs and tests
- All chart layout dimensions derived from config or content metrics
- All 10 theme presets produce readable, visually correct output for every diagram type
- No regression in golden snapshot tests (bless after fixes)

## Architecture Note
The fix strategy uses three approaches depending on context:
1. **Direct theme reference** (preferred): Pass `&theme.colors.X` where theme is in scope
2. **CSS custom properties** (for functions without theme param): Use `var(--fm-text-color, #fallback)` which the embedded theme CSS defines
3. **Config extension** (for layout dimensions): Add fields to LayoutSpacing or create diagram-specific config structs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:23.946145931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:10.864041670Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:10.863558044Z","close_reason":"All 12 subtasks completed: 6 hardcoded color fixes (SVG notes, canvas notes, band/axis labels, cluster dividers, diagram title, quadrant fills), 4 layout constant extractions (sequence spacing, pie/quadrant adaptive sizing, legend config), 1 canvas color audit, 1 theme regression test suite.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-1m4x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:47.492121152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-3v97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:48.385129031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-5slz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:46.541630975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-8im7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:45.613295965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-91wx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:52.057545344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-i138","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:44.682189940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-own9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:52.990352298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-qiyv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:49.299710035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-r5y9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:51.118245559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-sb52","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:50.227479214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-tl00","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:43.778156070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ma11","depends_on_id":"bd-u4c1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:42.874564768Z","crea -{"id":"bd-mcva","title":"Clean up parser test garbage files at repo root","description":"## Problem
Parser tests or malformed input handling occasionally creates garbage files at the repo root:
- B[🚀 (zero-byte mojibake file, created 2026-03-31)
- Potentially other B* files matching the parser test output pattern

These are artifacts of malformed diagram parsing that leak to the filesystem. The .gitignore now covers B[* and B*nend* patterns, so they won't be committed, but the files remain on disk.

## Scope
1. Delete the B[🚀 file (requires explicit user permission per AGENTS.md Rule 1).
2. Investigate WHERE in the parser these files are created. If the parser is writing to the filesystem during normal operation, that's a bug — parsers should never create files as a side effect of parsing.
3. If it's a test artifact, ensure the test cleans up after itself.

## Root Cause Investigation
The file appears to be created when a Mermaid input string starting with "B[" is interpreted as a file path by the CLI's input handling (stdin vs file detection). The CLI likely tries to open "B[🚀" as a file when it should be treating it as literal diagram text. This could be a real bug in input routing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Garbage file deleted.
- Root cause identified and documented (is it a CLI input routing bug or a test artifact?).
- If CLI bug: fix the input path detection to not create spurious files.
- If test artifact: test cleanup added.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:03.218997278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T18:37:07.717517567Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mcva","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:06.871321790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

B[* file was external artifact, .gitignore prevents commits.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mcva","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:06.871321790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r","title":"[FNX] EPIC: Graph Intelligence Integration via franken_networkx","description":"Background
frankenmermaid already has deterministic parse->layout->render pipelines. franken_networkx (fnx) provides a high-performance algorithm engine plus CGSE witness artifacts. Integrating the two can materially improve structural diagnostics, layout decision quality, and observability if we stage the rollout carefully.

Critical Constraint
Current Rust fnx algorithms are primarily Graph (undirected) centric. Immediate wins should target undirected structural analysis and diagnostics. Directed-layout replacement must be treated as a later phase gated by explicit DiGraph coverage in fnx.

Epic Goal
Add an accretive fnx-powered graph-intelligence layer to frankenmermaid that:
1) improves diagnostics and layout decisions,
2) preserves deterministic behavior,
3) exposes explainable evidence/witness artifacts,
4) avoids risky big-bang replacement of fm-layout internals.

Program Outcomes
- Better parser/CLI diagnostics (components, articulation points, bridges, cycle basis).
- Better layout tie-breaks and cycle-reversal scoring on dense/pathological graphs.
- Witness-aware telemetry for algorithmic decisions and complexity costs.
- Feature-gated rollout with deterministic parity checks and explicit success criteria.
- User-facing controls, semantic enhancements, and actionable recommendation UX.
- Runtime budget and fallback guardrails for reliable behavior on adversarial inputs.
- Comprehensive unit/integration/e2e verification with structured evidence logging.

Definition of Done
- All child issues complete with acceptance criteria and test evidence.
- Determinism checks pass repeatedly for representative corpus.
- Quality gates pass via rch offload for CPU-intensive commands:
  - `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
  - `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check`
  - `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace`
- CLI/WASM surfaces expose actionable fnx-derived diagnostics and decision traces.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:51:05.701089257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-14T09:32:42.960228746Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T09:32:42.960018628Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","diagnostics","fnx","layout","parser"]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.1","title":"[FNX] Integration architecture contract and phased risk matrix","description":"Goal
Create the authoritative technical contract for integrating franken_networkx (fnx) into frankenmermaid without destabilizing current deterministic behavior.

Background
The project already has deterministic parsing/layout/rendering in Rust. fnx provides strong graph algorithms and witness artifacts but current Rust APIs are largely undirected. We need a staged plan that captures immediate wins and blocks unsafe assumptions.

Scope
- Define hard boundaries between Phase 1 (undirected structural intelligence) and Phase 2 (directed algorithm adoption).
- Define success metrics for quality, latency, determinism, and diagnostics usefulness.
- Define explicit rollback conditions and kill-switch behavior via feature flags.
- Produce implementation map across crates: fm-core, fm-parser, fm-layout, fm-cli, fm-wasm.

Acceptance Criteria
- Written architecture contract embedded in repo docs and referenced by all child beads.
- Determinism contract and fallback behavior explicitly specified.
- Risk register includes algorithm mismatch, runtime overhead, and maintenance coupling.
- Clear go/no-go gates for each integration phase.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.873613155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:42:59.808188592Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:42:59.807996479Z","close_reason":"Extended FNX_INTEGRATION.md with full architecture contract: phase boundaries, success metrics, risk register (4 risks with mitigations), go/no-go gates, implementation map across crates, and evidence requirements.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","fnx","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:35.873613155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.1.1","title":"[FNX] Decide dependency model and feature-flag topology","description":"Goal
Decide exactly how frankenmermaid depends on fnx (workspace path, git dependency, or published crates) with reproducible builds and minimal coupling risk.

Scope
- Evaluate dependency strategies for local development, CI, and release packaging.
- Define feature flags (`fnx-integration`, `fnx-experimental-directed`) and default states.
- Specify how to build when fnx is unavailable.

Acceptance Criteria
- Cargo dependency strategy chosen and documented with rationale.
- Feature flags implemented in Cargo manifests with deterministic defaults.
- CI matrix includes fnx-on and fnx-off paths.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
